expectations.text: Uses paulista pronunciation (not carioca; closed Ê has no Y-glide like SÊY)
expectations.evidence: você respelled as 'vo-SEH' (no Y-glide). português as 'poor-too-GEHS'. Pure vowel quality, no diphthong.

expectations.text: All Portuguese words are real spellings — no invented entries like *éste*, no fake circumflex on words that lack one (e.g., not *SÊR* for ser)
expectations.evidence: Words listed: você, é, café, português, mesa, festa — all real spellings, no invented forms

expectations.text: Includes phonetic respellings for both É and Ê example words
expectations.evidence: Closed: vo-SEH, MEH-zah, poor-too-GEHS. Open: EH, ka-FEH, FEHS-tah.

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: você (you), é (is), café (coffee), português (Portuguese), mesa (table), festa (party) all glossed

expectations.text: Leads with the more familiar/higher-frequency sound (closed Ê), per the centrality principle
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'Lead with the closed sound. It's the one your English ear already hears in você and português, and it appears in the highest-frequency words.'

expectations.text: Contrast pairs share phonological category (one-claim-per-block: doesn't mix closed-stressed with closed-unstressed without flagging)
expectations.evidence: Sections are atomic: accented-tells-you, no-accent-still-quality, what-accents-aren't. mesa/festa contrast is within same context (stressed plain e, no accent mark)

expectations.text: Phonetic respellings are present and concrete (the respelling IS the teaching, not just labels like 'closed' / 'open')
expectations.evidence: Every example word includes its respelling alongside the label, not just the label alone

expectations.text: Stays within the open/closed E topic (does not balloon into unstressed E reduction, ser-conjugation, nasal vowels as primary content)
expectations.evidence: Tight scope: only stressed open/closed E. No detour into unstressed reduction, ser conjugation, or nasals.
